vulnerabilities.description: The backend accepts the is_admin form parameter and updates the role even though the UI disables this field. Posting name=...&is_admin=1 successfully switched the account to Admin (response showed the Admin option selected).
State-changing form has no CSRF token or protective header. Authentication relies on a cookie (SameSite=Lax), which does not guarantee CSRF protection in all contexts.
Public API documentation and OpenAPI spec are exposed, revealing endpoints and request/response schemas.
Responses lack common security headers (e.g., Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options), increasing risk of clickjacking and content injection.
